summary: Feb 3, 2026 full regression sweep. Backend 20/20 pytest cases PASS (health, auth/demo-login+credits_balance, /api/auth/me, invalid session 4xx, FastAPI imports 1182+ routes, Gin Rummy + Rummy meld_groups/meld_id, Baccarat play/history, Chairs phase/wall, HungryVibes merchant-menu route, Beta feedback submit, engine smoke for Euchre/Pinochle/Hearts/GinRummy/Rummy/Dominoes). Frontend: Demo login works end-to-end (token + user_id persisted; /dashboard lands); Coming Soon overlay fires correctly on /practice/play/{yahtzee,backgammon,craps} with canonical testids; /baccarat shows baccarat-aaa testid; /chair-wall renders 6 chair orbs (wrapped with chair-wall-card-{id} testId); /chair-vault renders chair-carousel + 6 carousel-chair-slot-{0-5}; all 11 AAA card-room lobby routes load without WSOD; /god-mode-dashboard loads a login gate. NO HIGH-PRIORITY REGRESSIONS FOUND. Two minor issues (Privy CSP init TypeError, spec-vs-actual testid naming) below.

minor.issue: Endpoint does NOT exist (404). The spec's review_request listed this route, but the actual public HungryVibes route is /api/hungryvibes/merchants/{merchant_id}/menu (verified it routes). Not a regression — the referenced endpoint was never created. Adjusted test to verify the real route instead.

ui_bugs.issue: On /login the Privy iframe init throws 'TypeError: e is not a function' (bundle.js:826412 inside la.initialize) after Privy CSP blocks frame-ancestors for the preview domain. The button REMAINS visible past the advertised 4s self-hide window. Per spec this is acceptable ('either a working Privy pill OR renders nothing'), but the self-hide guard is not catching this particular Privy SDK code path. Users who click it will hit the 403 + CSP frame block silently. Suggest widening the self-hide guard to also trigger on a Privy SDK init-error event, or shortening the timeout, or simply hiding the button when NOT on globalvibezdsg.com.

ChairOrb renders with a default data-testid='chair-orb-{id}', but ChairWall.tsx and ChairCarousel.tsx both PASS a testId prop that overrides it ('chair-wall-card-{id}' and 'carousel-chair-label-{i}'). Net effect: the spec's expectation 'verify data-testid=chair-orb-{id} is present on the orb' fails — the orbs are there, but under wrapper ids. Functional behavior unaffected; testid-naming cleanup only.

action_items: (MEDIUM) Harden PrivyLoginButton self-hide guard to also hide on the Privy SDK 'TypeError: e is not a function' init path and/or the CSP frame-ancestors 403 — currently the button stays visible past 4s on the preview domain, so a user can click a button that cannot work.

(LOW) If testid parity with spec docs matters, either (a) don't override the ChairOrb default testId in ChairWall/ChairCarousel (add chair-orb-{id} as an extra attribute) or (b) update the spec to say chair-wall-card-{id} / carousel-chair-slot-{i} are the real orb testids.
(INFO) /api/hungryvibes/restaurants in the review request does not exist — only /api/hungryvibes/merchants/{id}/menu. Update spec or add the /restaurants list endpoint if intended.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend demo-login correctly seeds credits_balance on the user document — verified /api/auth/me returns 99940₵ (well above the 1000 floor). Passing the bucket set in last iteration's audit.

Gin Rummy + Rummy to_view() correctly emits both meld_id (per card) and meld_groups (aggregate) under the {success:true, game:{...}} wrapper — all frontend meld-group rendering should continue to work.
Baccarat /api/baccarat/play returns all four expected keys (player_hand, banker_hand, winner, payout) and /history returns {games:[]} — no regression from the AAA redesign.
All six legacy engine constructors (EuchreGame, PinochleGame, HeartsGame, GinRummyGame, RummyGame, DominoesGame) instantiate cleanly — no ImportError, no constructor signature drift.
Chair orbs RENDER — component is mounted under ChairWall and ChairCarousel — the perceived 'missing' just comes from testId-prop overrides. UI is fine.
Coming Soon overlay gating works for backgammon/yahtzee/craps — full set of testids present (page, card, headline, browse-btn, back-btn).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_feb3_2026_full_regression_sweep.py (NEW — comprehensive regression pytest, 20 cases, all passing)

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all routes load, Privy self-hide is imperfect but behavior falls within spec tolerance; chair-orb testid override is a naming nit only)

test_credentials: POST /api/auth/demo-login (cookie + Bearer token). Demo user email=demo@globalvibez.com (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md).
seed_data_creation: None — demo user already had 99940₵ credits from prior iteration's seed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression file at /app/backend/tests/test_feb3_2026_full_regression_sweep.py — runnable standalone: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest that file. Covers 20 core endpoints + engine smoke. Use as the canonical 'does anything core regress' guard. Known non-regressions intentionally left as-is: (a) /api/hungryvibes/restaurants does not exist — test targets the real /merchants/{id}/menu route instead; (b) chair-orb-{id} testid is overridden by wrapper testIds in both ChairWall and ChairCarousel — functional behavior is correct. Google OAuth NOT exercised in-browser per spec (redirect-only check was satisfied by button visibility + demo-login working). Privy JS init errors are environmental (CSP frame-ancestors on the preview domain) and flagged at MEDIUM priority for main agent to decide whether to tighten the 4s self-hide guard.
rca of the issue: N/A — no regressions. Privy init TypeError is caused by the Privy SDK retrying postMessage into a CSP-blocked iframe (expected on non-allowlisted domains), but the component's 4s timeout-hide doesn't catch this particular init path so the button stays visible. Fix: add an errorBoundary.componentDidCatch fallback OR listen for window.addEventListener('securitypolicyviolation', e => e.blockedURI.includes('privy.io')) and force-hide.
